items.title: Arsenal v Sunderland, Wolves v Chelsea, Burnley v West Ham and more: football – live
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League and EFL updates from the 3pm GMT kick-offs ⚽ Live scores | Tables | Follow us on Bluesky | Mail Emillia Full-time: Manchester United 2-0 Tottenham Carrick earns his fourth successive win as United’s interim head coach. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: Roefs incasseert tweede tegentreffer tegen Arsenal
items.summary: Zaterdag staan er vier Eredivisie-wedstrijden op het programma, maar ook in het buitenland wordt gevoetbald. Onder meer Manchester United Arsenal, Barcelona, Borussia Dortmund en Napoli komen in actie. Volg al het voetbal in de VI Live.

items.title: Dortmund ontsnapt tegen basisdebutant Shiogai en legt druk weer bij Bayern
items.summary: Borussia Dortmund leek zaterdag op weg naar een nieuwe knauw in de Duitse titelrace, maar in extremis wonnen Die Borussen alsnog op bezoek bij VfL Wolfsburg, dat moet vrezen voor degradatie.

items.title: Viral Utd fan hopeful of haircut: It's 'going soon'!
items.summary: Viral Manchester United supporter Frank Illet his long hair is "going soon" -- over a year after he started a challenge not to have it cut until his side won five matches in a row.

items.title: Palmer breaks Chelsea's all-time hat trick record
items.summary: Cole Palmer moved clear of some Chelsea legends on Saturday as he scored his fourth trick for the team, setting a new club record.

items.title: USMNT to compete for 1 of 6 spots at 2030 WC
items.summary: Concacaf says it will have six automatic qualifying spots in the 2030 World Cup and a seventh will be available as part of an intercontinental playoff.

items.title: Barcelona confirm withdrawal from Super League
items.summary: Barcelona have announced their official withdrawal from the European Super League in the latest blow to a project which has struggled to get off the ground.

items.title: Man Utd beat 10-man Spurs for 4th straight win
items.summary: Manchester United's revival under Michael Carrick rolled on against Tottenham with goals from Bryan Mbeumo and Bruno Fernandes piling more misery on Thomas Frank's Spurs.

items.title: Tutti Quanti has Nicholls dreaming big after Newbury demolition job
items.summary: Paul Nicholls is contemplating supplementing Tutti Quanti for the Champion Hurdle after an astounding performance in the William Hill Hurdle at Newbury.

items.title: Tottenham-keeper wordt filosofisch na vragen over degradatiestrijd
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur won dit seizoen zeven wedstrijden in de Premier League, maar staat inmiddels op een reeks van zeven duels zonder zege en won zelfs maar twee van de laatste zestien competitieduels. Gaat Tottenham zo door, moet het echt oppassen en naar onderen kijken. Keeper Guglielmo Vicario wil het niet zo somber inzien.

items.title: Superserie Summerville duurt voort: man in vorm slaat wéér toe
items.summary: Crysencio Summerville is zonder twijfel aan een van de beste periodes in zijn carrière bezig. De 24-jarige aanvaller van West Ham United scoorde zaterdagmiddag uit tegen Burnley opnieuw en is nu in zijn laatste vijf optredens trefzeker geweest.

items.title: Romero cost Tottenham, not the manager - Sutton
items.summary: Jason Mohammad, Chris Sutton and Nedum Onuoha discuss Tottenham captain Cristian Romero's red card against Manchester United at Old Trafford and the impact it had on the game.

items.title: Romero bood excuses aan bij teamgenoten na rode kaart tegen Man Utd
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur-verdediger Cristian Romero benadeelde zijn team tegen Manchester United en heeft daarvoor zijn excuses aangeboden. Dit vertelde manager Thomas Frank na de nederlaag op Old Trafford.

items.title: Oxlade-Chamberlain joins Celtic
items.summary: Celtic have signed former Arsenal and Liverpool midfielder Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain.

items.title: Fatigued Raducanu suffers defeat in Transylvania Open final
items.summary: Emma Raducanu's hopes of winning her maiden WTA Tour title were ended in the Transylvania Open final by veteran Sorana Cirstea.

items.title: Lulamba cements Arkle favouritism with Game Spirit Chase win
items.summary: Lulamba cemented his position at the head of the betting for the Arkle at Cheltenham when ultimately running out a convincing winner against his elders in the William Hill Game Spirit Chase at Newbury.

items.title: Championship roundup: Davis sinks Derby to boost Ipswich’s promotion charge
items.summary: Derby captain Travis scores own goal and sees red Southampton extend Watford’s woeful run Leif Davis’s superb late header gave Ipswich a 2-1 victory at Derby to strengthen their promotion challenge. The visitors took the lead in the eighth minute when the Derby captain, Lewis Travis, headed a corner into his own net but Derby hit back in the second half and drew level through Rhian Brewster’s penalty. But Davis had the final word and the home side ended the match with 10 men after Travis’s miserable afternoon ended with him sent off in added time for a second yellow card. Continue reading...

items.title: Fernandes keeps Carrick’s perfect Manchester United record intact after Romero red card
items.summary: Michael Carrick’s Manchester United revival has a fourth consecutive league win, while Thomas Frank’s Tottenham sink further, with still no victory in the competition in 2026. Bruno Fernandes’s 81st-minute strike was a feathered touch off a shin – from Diogo Dalot’s cross – that beat Guglielmo Vicario to the left of the Spurs goalkeeper, and confirmed a miserable day for Frank. “He is our captain and one of our important players,” the Dane had said after retaining Cristian Romero as captain despite the defender’s “disgraceful” outburst regarding there being only 11 outfield players available for last Sunday’s draw with Manchester City. Continue reading...

items.title: Final Score
items.summary: Jason Mohammad and guests bring you all the goals, analysis, reaction and interviews.

items.title: Barcelona withdraw from European Super League project
items.summary: Barcelona withdraw from the European Super League project, leaving rivals Real Madrid as the only one of the original 12 clubs still signed up to the plans.
